incorrects.description: Student selected 36 — counting by 1s instead of recognizing the scale of 5

params.description: Axis numbers highlight to draw attention to the scale pattern.

steps.text: Look at the numbers on the axis. They don't go 31, 32, 33. They jump by 5s. What comes after 30? After 35?

incorrects.description: Student selected 45 — skipped ahead, missing the next value in the sequence

params.description: Axis numbers highlight to draw attention to the sequence.

steps.text: 45 is on the axis, but it's not right after 35. Count from 30: 30, then add 5. What do you get?

incorrects.description: Student selected 30 — went backward instead of forward in the sequence

params.description: Axis numbers highlight to draw attention to the direction.

steps.text: 30 comes before 35, not after. The axis counts up: 0, 5, 10, 15... What's the next jump after 35?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

steps.text: Here's how the axis works. It counts by 5s: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35. After 35, add 5 more. That gives us 40. The answer is 40. That's how a scale of 5 works on a bar graph axis.
